text: Open Settings to configure the app, permissions and language.
Settings groups app-wide options: the permissions the app needs, the interface language, and analytics consent. Nothing here starts or stops the server.
Show a QR code so a nearby device can join and open your library.
The QR encodes the Wi-Fi network (or hotspot) plus the address of your library. Guests scan it to connect and browse from their device — no typing required.
Switch between light and dark theme.
The installed app version.
Indicator of the current status and a shortcut to the Wi-Fi interface.
Shows whether the Wi-Fi interface is up and is also a shortcut to it. When it is up, other devices on the same network can open your library at your phone's address.
Indicator of the current status and a shortcut to the hotspot.
Shows whether the phone's hotspot is on and is also a shortcut to it. When it is on, connected devices can join it and open your library.
Open your library in the built-in browser.
Start or stop the content server.
Other connected devices can only reach your library while the server is running. The first start can take a few seconds.
Advanced server settings.
Enable custom DNS settings for the server.
Only needed in special network setups; leave it off unless you know you need it.
Apply the DNS settings you entered.
Show the connection log.
Clear the connection log.
Copy the whole log to the clipboard.
Choose Share to send your library or apps to another device.
Choose Receive to get content from another device.
Transfer over Wi-Fi.
Transfer over the hotspot.
Start the server so another device can pull your library.
Share the K2Go app itself with a nearby device.
Lets someone install K2Go from you without internet: they connect and download the app directly.
Scan a QR to connect to the other device.
Cancel the current transfer.
Flip to the next set of gauges.
Show or hide the list of installed modules (shown as LED indicators).
Turn anonymous usage analytics on or off.
When on, the app shares anonymous usage data to help improve it. No personal content is collected.
Pick the type of feedback you're sending.
Send your feedback to the team.
Trigger a test crash — for diagnostics only.
Continue to the next setup step.
Allow notifications so the app can alert you about the server and transfers.
Allow storage access so the app can read and serve your library files.
Allow the app to display over other apps (needed for some on-screen help).
Let the app keep running in the background so the server stays up.
Open the system screen to manage all permissions at once.
Choose the app and content language.
Language of the app interface.
Preferred language for downloaded content.
Finish and close settings.
Go to the setup steps.
Go to feedback.
Go to the About screen.
Show the navigation bar.
Hide the navigation bar.
Go back.
Go to the library home page.
Reload the page.
Close the browser and return to the app.
Go forward.
Switch the QR between the Wi-Fi network and the hotspot.
Close the QR code.
Dismiss this crash report without sending.
Send the crash report to help us fix the problem.
Status and control to disable the child-process restriction.
Status and control to disable the Phantom Process Killer (PPK).
Show advanced monitoring for the install.
Connect over ADB for advanced monitoring.
Install the Basic edition (smallest set of apps).

The Basic edition installs the essentials:
  • Kiwix — offline Wikipedia and other libraries
  • IIAB Maps — curated offline maps from Internet-in-a-Box
  • System Dashboard — manage content and status
Install the Standard edition.

The Standard edition adds Kolibri to the Basic set:
  • Kiwix — offline Wikipedia and other libraries
  • IIAB Maps — curated offline maps from Internet-in-a-Box
  • System Dashboard — manage content and status
  • Kolibri — structured courses and lessons
Install the Full edition (largest set of apps).

The Full edition installs our most powerful applications for everyday use:
  • Kiwix — offline Wikipedia and other libraries
  • Kolibri — structured courses and lessons
  • IIAB Maps — curated offline maps from Internet-in-a-Box
  • System Dashboard — manage content and status
  • Calibre-Web — e-books library
  • Code on the Go — a full offline-first IDE for building applications
Also install companion content with the modules.
Open Kiwix download settings (language and variant).
Provision the selected edition now.
Delete the installed content.
Show or hide module management.
Refresh the list of modules installed on the system.
Launch the installation of the selected modules.
Show or hide maintenance tools.
Back up your installed content and settings.
Reset to a bare-bones system install (no services).
Restore from a backup.

Import a backup from external storage.
Choose which backup to use.
Close the restore log.
Switch manually between light and dark, or let the app follow your device's system theme.
Turn on a local-only hotspot that works even with no SIM.
Creates a Wi-Fi hotspot without using the carrier tethering path, so it works with no SIM or mobile plan — useful when the normal hotspot is blocked. Location must be on.
Show a Wi-Fi QR to join the local-only hotspot.
The local-only hotspot uses an auto-generated name and password; scanning the QR joins it without typing them.
Not a simple on/off — it raises Android's phantom-process limit (to 256) so the server's child processes aren't killed.
Indicator of the phone's internet access.
Gray = not checked yet, red = no internet, green = online.
Indicator of whether Developer mode is enabled.
Developer options are effectively required for advanced features like sharing or transferring an install and wireless debugging.
Export a backup to external storage.
Delete a saved backup.
